title: Differential testing framework and benchmark suite
description:
Build the testing infrastructure to validate correctness against C tree-sitter and measure performance.

Deliverables:
- Differential testing harness: for each grammar, parse a corpus with both C tree-sitter (via CLI or CGo test helper) and Go implementation. Compare S-expression output, node byte ranges, and query match results.
- Use tree-sitter test corpus (test/corpus/ in each grammar repo) as primary validation set.
- Benchmark suite: parse corpus of files at various sizes (1KB, 10KB, 100KB, 1MB) across JSON, Go, JavaScript, Python.
- Incremental parse benchmarks: single char edit, line insert, multi-line edit at various file sizes.
- Memory profiling integration: report allocations per parse, GC pause times.
- Comparison benchmarks: same files through C tree-sitter and Go, report ratio with confidence intervals.
- CI integration: run differential tests and benchmarks on every commit.

Tests:
- The framework itself is the test — validate against C tree-sitter across all supported grammars
- Benchmark reproducibility (low coefficient of variation)

Reference: docs/design.md §8 Profiling Strategy, §10 Risk: Parse Tree Compatibility, docs/testing-strategy.md
